key_insight: A Lagrangian trajectory-based scheme reconstructs polymeric stress from deformation-gradient history, avoiding full Eulerian constitutive solves in steady viscoelastic flows.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical stress mapping from experimentally measured velocity fields, improving analysis of complex viscoelastic microflows and obstacle-laden channels.
method: Integrate deformation-gradient history along fluid-element trajectories in a known steady velocity field.
Reconstruct polymeric stress for FENE-P and Oldroyd-B without solving the full transported conformation-tensor equation.
Apply and validate the scheme on canonical flows and channel flows past circular obstacles using simulation and microfluidic data.
evidence_signal: The reconstructed stress fields agree with traditional Eulerian reference solutions in both numerical simulations and microfluidic experiments.
watch_item: The approach relies on known, steady velocity fields, so accuracy may degrade for strongly unsteady or poorly resolved experimental flows.
paper_title: Lagrangian evaluation of polymeric stress in viscoelastic fluids
